nodes.name: Precision Agriculture
nodes.description: Farming management approach using GPS, sensors, and analytics for site-specific optimization.

nodes.name: Variable Rate Technology
nodes.description: Technology that applies inputs (fertilizer, pesticides) at variable rates across a field.

nodes.name: Crop Yield
nodes.description: Amount of agricultural production per unit area. Affected by soil, weather, pests, and management.

nodes.name: Soil Health
nodes.description: Condition of soil including pH, organic matter, microbiome diversity affecting plant growth.

nodes.name: Byzantine Fault Tolerance
nodes.description: Ability of a distributed system to reach consensus despite Byzantine (arbitrary) failures.

nodes.name: Byzantine Generals Problem
nodes.description: Fundamental problem in distributed computing formulated by Lamport, Shostak, Pease (1982).

nodes.name: PBFT
nodes.description: Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance by Castro and Liskov (1999). Three-phase consensus protocol.

nodes.name: Defense of Necessity
nodes.description: Criminal law defense arguing the act prevented a greater harm. Requires imminent threat, no alternative, proportionality.

nodes.name: Duress
nodes.description: Criminal defense involving coercion by another person, distinct from necessity.

nodes.name: Model Penal Code
nodes.description: Statutory text by the American Law Institute. Section 3.02 codifies the choice of evils defense.

edges.description: Precision agriculture aims to maximize crop yield through data-driven management.
VRT is a key technology in precision agriculture.
Soil health directly impacts crop productivity and yield.
BFT solutions address the Byzantine Generals Problem.
PBFT is a practical implementation of Byzantine Fault Tolerance.
Necessity and duress are related but distinct defenses in criminal law.
MPC Section 3.02 codifies the choice of evils / necessity defense.
